patches.reason: Generator emitted duplicate `var bodyClassSessionClassroomName string` declarations in 4 reservation request-body command files, breaking compilation. Spec has both `classroom.name` (nested) and `classroom_name` (flat alias) on the reservation body schema; the codegen walked both into the same Go identifier.
patches.change: Removed the second consecutive `var bodyClassSessionClassroomName string` declaration in each file (line 30).

patches.reason: Same root cause as dedup-classroom-name-vars: duplicate `cmd.Flags().StringVar(&bodyClassSessionClassroomName, "class-session-classroom-name", "", "Classroom name")` registration would panic Cobra at command init on duplicate flag.
patches.change: Removed the duplicate StringVar registration with description "Classroom name" in each file. Kept the registration with description "Name".

patches.reason: Generator marked every nested body field as required on `me reservations-create`, but the live API only requires {reservation_type, class_session.id, payment_option.id} (and payment_option is optional for waitlist). Discovered during production booking test: the CLI rejected reservations-create with --class-session-id alone, demanding 50+ unrelated flags. Empirically validated the minimal body shape against the real /me/reservations endpoint.
patches.change: Replaced the 550-line `if !stdinBody { ... }` block (lines 209-759 in the generated file) with a 3-rule validator: default reservation_type to standard; enforce enum standard|standby|waitlist; require class-session-id with a hint pointing to `classes list`; require payment-option-id (except for waitlist) with a hint pointing to `classes payment-options <id>`. Defaults and validation now fire in dry-run too so the preview body reflects what would actually be sent.

patches.reason: Config field `oauth_authorization` stored the raw token and `AuthHeader()` returned it verbatim — the upstream Customer API requires `Bearer <token>` and returned 401 when the prefix was missing. Discovered during production test: had to manually set `auth_header = "Bearer <token>"` in config.toml because the more natural `oauth_authorization = "<token>"` produced silent auth failures.
patches.change: Patched `AuthHeader()` to auto-prefix `Bearer ` when the source is `oauth_authorization`, with a pass-through for tokens that already include a `Bearer ` or `Token ` scheme. `auth_header` continues to be sent verbatim.

patches.reason: Mariana Tek's Customer API does not offer a self-serve token endpoint for end users — OAuth registration requires going through <vendor-integrations-email>. The only practical auth path for individual members is to extract the bearer token from the iframe widget's session cookie. The generator's default `auth setup` text pointed at RFC 6750 (the bearer-token RFC) but offered no concrete extraction steps.
patches.change: Added a new `auth from-browser` subcommand that parses the cookie value `{expires, tokenData: {accessToken, refreshToken, tokenType, scope, expiresIn}}` users copy from DevTools, then writes the access token to config under oauth_authorization (the auto-Bearer-prefix patch handles the wire format). Updated `auth setup` to print Mariana-Tek-specific extraction steps instead of the generic RFC 6750 message.

patches.reason: Same root cause as marianatek-required-flag-bloat, but extended across the remaining 18 create commands the generator produced. After the production booking test surfaced the over-required pattern on `me reservations-create`, an audit of the other POST endpoints found the same spec-derived require-storm — totalling ~2300 lines of false-positive required-flag checks. Verified with a sample call: `me credit-cards-create` with no args now returns the API's actual 422 ("number is required, usage_location is required") instead of the spec's invented requirements.
patches.change: Replaced each `if !stdinBody { ... 100s of required-flag checks ... }` block with an empty PATCH-commented block that lets the API surface real-required fields via 422 errors. ~2300 lines removed. `me reservations-create` keeps its bespoke 3-rule validator (different patch above).

patches.reason: Generator emitted a generic example `marianatek-pp-cli me reservations-create --booked-by example-value` based on a server-derived field. Misleading for new users.
patches.change: Replaced the generic example with three real-world variants: book-with-payment, dry-run preview, and waitlist join. Imported `strings` to use `strings.Trim` per the framework's convention for multi-line examples.

patches.reason: `watch --auto-book` posts to `/me/reservations`, so advertising the shell-out MCP tool as read-only can bypass host write confirmation guardrails.
patches.change: Removed the `mcp:read-only` annotation from `watch` while keeping the command exposed as a novel MCP shell-out tool.

patches.reason: `watch --auto-book` and `book-regular --auto` used the live-proven JSONAPI reservation body, while `me reservations-create` still sent generated flat/nested fields for the same endpoint.
patches.change: Added a shared reservation body helper that emits `{data:{type:"reservation",attributes:{class_session_id,...}}}` and normalized the generated reservations-create body into that envelope.

patches.reason: Generated typed store tables declare resource-specific parent columns as NOT NULL, but dependent-resource sync injects the generic `parent_id`; generic generated upsert tests also exercise id-only rows.
patches.change: Added a helper that fills required parent columns from the resource-specific field, then `parent_id`, then an empty string fallback so dependent typed upserts do not fail before storing the JSON payload.

upstream_followups.summary: OpenAPI nested-vs-flat alias for same property emits duplicate Go declarations + duplicate Cobra flag bindings
upstream_followups.where: Generator codegen for request-body fields (probably internal/codegen/cli/body_flags.go) walks both `classroom.name` and `classroom_name` into the same Go identifier without deduplication.
upstream_followups.evidence: All 4 reservation request-body commands hit the same dup; classroom.name + classroom_name in the Reservation request schema is the trigger.

upstream_followups.summary: OpenAPI request body marked every nested field required, but the live API treats most as optional or server-derived
upstream_followups.where: The Mariana Tek Customer API's UserReservation schema (and likely many other vendor specs) defines a flat property required list that includes derived/readonly fields. The generator should distinguish writable-required from readonly-required, or default to optional when in doubt.
upstream_followups.evidence: /me/reservations real-API minimal body = {reservation_type, class_session: {id}, payment_option: {id}}. Generator emitted 50+ required flags from the spec.

upstream_followups.summary: When a spec declares `bearer auth` with the standard security scheme, the generated AuthHeader() should auto-prefix `Bearer ` for the raw-token storage field.
upstream_followups.where: config-template in the generator. Today the AuthHeader() function returns the raw token unchanged.
upstream_followups.evidence: Spec-declared `OAuth Authorization` security scheme used bearer scheme. Users storing a raw token in oauth_authorization got 401s. Workaround: set auth_header = "Bearer <token>" verbatim.
